name: pyvda

metadata.name: pyvda
metadata.summary: Python implementation of the VirtualDesktopAccessor for manipulating Windows 10 virtual desktops.

documentation:
# Python Virtual Desktop Accessor
Python module providing programmatic access to most of the settings accessed through the [Windows task view](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Task_View).
Including switching virtual desktops, moving windows between virtual desktops, pinning windows and listing the windows on a desktop.

Originally based on https://github.com/Ciantic/VirtualDesktopAccessor.

## Installation
```
pip install pyvda
```

## Example usage
```python
from pyvda import AppView, get_apps_by_z_order, VirtualDesktop, get_virtual_desktops

number_of_active_desktops = len(get_virtual_desktops())
print(f"There are {number_of_active_desktops} active desktops")

current_desktop = VirtualDesktop.current()
print(f"Current desktop is number {current_desktop}")

current_window = AppView.current()
target_desktop = VirtualDesktop(5)
current_window.move(target_desktop)
print(f"Moved window {current_window.hwnd} to {target_desktop.number}")

print("Going to desktop number 5")
VirtualDesktop(5).go()

print("Pinning the current window")
AppView.current().pin()
```

## Documentation
Full API documentation can be found at [Read the Docs](https://pyvda.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)

functions.name: pyvda.pyvda.get_apps_by_z_order
documentation:
Get a list of AppViews, ordered by their Z position, with
the foreground window first.

Args:
    switcher_windows (bool, optional): Only include windows which appear in the alt-tab dialogue. Defaults to True.
    current_desktop (bool, optional): Only include windows which are on the current virtual desktop. Defaults to True.

Returns:
    List[AppView]: AppViews matching the specified criteria.

functions.name: pyvda.pyvda.get_virtual_desktops
documentation:
Return a list of all current virtual desktops, one for each desktop visible in the task view.

Returns:
    List[VirtualDesktop]: Virtual desktops currently active.

functions.name: pyvda.pyvda.set_wallpaper_for_all_desktops
documentation:
Set wallpaper on current virtual desktop to `path`.

Args:
    path (str): path to wallpaper file

classes.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.__init__
documentation:
One of the following parameters must be provided:

Args:
    hwnd (int, optional): Handle to a window. Defaults to None.
    view (IApplicationView, optional): An `IApplicationView` object. Defaults to None.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.hwnd
documentation: This window's handle.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.app_id
documentation: The ID of this window's app. Some specific types of windows do not have an app ID, and will return `None`.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.current
documentation:
Returns:
    AppView: An AppView for the currently focused window.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.is_shown_in_switchers
documentation: Is the view shown in the alt-tab view?
IApplicationView methods

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.is_visible
documentation: Is the view visible?

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.get_activation_timestamp
documentation: Get the last activation timestamp for this window.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.set_focus
documentation: Focus the window

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.switch_to
documentation:
Switch to the window. Behaves slightly differently to set_focus -
this is what is called when you use the alt-tab menu.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.pin
documentation: Pin the window (corresponds to the 'show window on all desktops' toggle).
IVirtualDesktopPinnedApps methods

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.unpin
documentation: Unpin the window (corresponds to the 'show window on all desktops' toggle).

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.is_pinned
documentation:
Check if this window is pinned (corresponds to the 'show window on all desktops' toggle).

Returns:
    bool: is the window pinned?

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.pin_app
documentation: Pin this window's app (corresponds to the 'show windows from this app on all desktops' toggle).

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.unpin_app
documentation: Unpin this window's app (corresponds to the 'show windows from this app on all desktops' toggle).

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.is_app_pinned
documentation:
Check if this window's app is pinned (corresponds to the 'show windows from this app on all desktops' toggle).

Returns:
    bool: is the app pinned?.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.move
documentation:
Move the window to a different virtual desktop.

Args:
    desktop (VirtualDesktop): Desktop to move the window to.

Example:

        >>> AppView.current().move_to_desktop(VirtualDesktop(1))
IVirtualDesktopManagerInternal methods

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.desktop_id
documentation:
Returns:
    GUID -- The ID of the desktop which the window is on.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.desktop
documentation:
Returns:
    VirtualDesktop: The virtual desktop which this window is on.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.is_on_desktop
documentation:
Is this window on the passed virtual desktop?

Args:
    desktop (VirtualDesktop): Desktop to check
    include_pinned (bool, optional): Also return `True` for pinned windows

Example:

    >>> AppView.current().is_on_desktop(VirtualDesktop(1))

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.AppView.is_on_current_desktop
documentation: Is this window on the current desktop?

A wrapper around an `IApplicationView` object exposing window functionality relating to:

    * Setting focus
    * Pinning and unpinning (making a window persistent across all virtual desktops)
    * Moving a window between virtual desktops

classes.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.__init__
documentation:
One of the following arguments must be provided:

Args:
    number (int, optional): The number of the desired desktop in the task view (1-indexed). Defaults to None.
    desktop_id (GUID, optional): A desktop GUID. Defaults to None.
    desktop (IVirtualDesktop, optional): An `IVirtualDesktop`. Defaults to None.
    current (bool, optional): The current virtual desktop. Defaults to False.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.current
documentation:
Convenience method to return a `VirtualDesktop` object for the
currently active desktop.

Returns:
    VirtualDesktop: The current desktop.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.create
documentation:
Create a new virtual desktop.

Returns:
    VirtualDesktop: The created desktop.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.id
documentation:
The GUID of this desktop.

Returns:
    GUID: The unique id for this desktop.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.number
documentation:
The index of this virtual desktop in the task view. Between 1 and
the total number of desktops active.

Returns:
    int: The desktop number.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.name
documentation:
The name of this virtual desktop in the task view.
Note that the default name is an empty string even though the task view shows
e.g. 'Desktop 4'.

Returns:
    str: The desktop name.

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: If the Windows version is < 19041.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.rename
documentation:
Rename this desktop.

Args:
    name: The new name for this desktop.

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: If the Windows version is < 19041.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.remove
documentation:
Delete this virtual desktop, falling back to 'fallback'.

Args:
    fallback (VirtualDesktop, optional): If you are currently on the desktop
    you pass to this method, focus will be shifted to the desktop passed here.
    If no desktop is passed, it will default to the first.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.go
documentation:
Switch to this virtual desktop.

Args:
    allow_set_foreground (bool, optional): Call AllowSetForegroundWindow(ASFW_ANY) before switching. This partially fixes an issue where the focus remains behind after switching. Defaults to True.

Note:
    More details at https://github.com/Ciantic/VirtualDesktopAccessor/issues/4 and https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/winuser/nf-winuser-allowsetforegroundwindow.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.apps_by_z_order
documentation:
Get a list of AppViews, ordered by their Z position, with
the foreground window first.

Args:
    switcher_windows (bool, optional): Only include windows which appear in the alt-tab dialogue. Defaults to True.
    current_desktop (bool, optional): Only include windows which are on the current virtual desktop. Defaults to True.

Returns:
    List[AppView]: AppViews matching the specified criteria.

methods.name: pyvda.pyvda.VirtualDesktop.set_wallpaper
documentation:
Set wallpaper on current virtual desktop to `path`.

Args:
    path (str): path to wallpaper file
Wrapper around the `IVirtualDesktop` COM object, representing one virtual desktop.

modules.name: pyvda
documentation:
# Python Virtual Desktop Accessor

Implements a subset of the functionality offered by <https://github.com/Ciantic/VirtualDesktopAccessor>, written in pure Python and installable via pip.

## Installation

```shell
$ pip install pyvda
```

## Example

```python
from pyvda import AppView, get_apps_by_z_order, VirtualDesktop, get_virtual_desktops

number_of_active_desktops = len(get_virtual_desktops())
print(f"There are {number_of_active_desktops} active desktops")

current_desktop = VirtualDesktop.current()
print(f"Current desktop is number {current_desktop}")

current_window = AppView.current()
target_desktop = VirtualDesktop(5)
current_window.move(target_desktop)
print(f"Moved window {current_window.hwnd} to {target_desktop.number}")

print("Going to desktop number 5")
VirtualDesktop(5).go()

print("Pinning the current window")
AppView.current().pin()
```

References:
    * GUIDs, Interfaces: https://github.com/Ciantic/VirtualDesktopAccessor/blob/master/VirtualDesktopAccessor/Win10Desktops.h

    * Object array: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/objectarray/nn-objectarray-iobjectarray
    * HSTRING: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/uwp/cpp-ref-for-winrt/hstring
    * comtypes: http://svn.python.org/projects/ctypes/tags/comtypes-0.3.2/docs/com_interfaces.html
    * dev blogs:
        * http://grabacr.net/archives/5601
        * https://www.cyberforum.ru/blogs/105416/blog3671.html
